title: Expressing Opinion
description: Learn how to express opinions using musta/mielestä and talk about likes and dislikes

questions.text: What does 'musta' mean in the context of opinions?
questions.explanation: 'Musta' means 'in my opinion' when expressing opinions. Note: it also means 'black,' but context makes it clear which meaning is intended.

questions.text: Which pronouns can you add -sta/-stä directly to?
options: You must use pronoun stems: mulla → musta, sulla → susta
You never use -sta/-stä with personal pronouns
questions.explanation: You can't add -sta to 'mä' directly. You must use the pronoun STEM: mulla → musta, sulla → susta, sillä → siitä.

questions.text: How do you say 'In his opinion, coffee is good'? Use the -sta/-stä method.
questions.explanation: Take the pronoun stem 'sillä' → add -sta → siitä. 'Siitä kahvi on hyvää' expresses his opinion about coffee.

options: Genitive form + mielestä = another way to say 'in [person]'s opinion'
questions.explanation: 'Mielestä' comes from 'mieli' (mind/opinion). Formula: Genitive + mielestä. Example: Mun mielestä = In my opinion.

questions.text: Express 'In my opinion, sauna is wonderful' using the mielestä method.
questions.explanation: Take genitive (mun/minun) + mielestä + opinion. 'Mun mielestä sauna on ihana' = In my opinion, sauna is wonderful.

questions.text: With a name like Lisa, how do you form the opinion construction?
questions.explanation: Names need genitive form. Lisa → Lisan (genitive). Then: Lisan mielestä = In Lisa's opinion.

questions.text: What case must follow 'tykkää' and 'pitää' (to like)?
questions.explanation: Both tykkää and pidät ALWAYS require the -sta/-stä suffix. Example: Mä tykkään kahvista (I like coffee). Mistä tykkäät? (What do you like?)

questions.text: Write two sentences: 'I like chocolate' and 'I like coffee' using both tykkää and pidät.
questions.explanation: Both work identically: 'Mä tykkään suklaasta' and 'Mä pidän suklaasta' mean the same thing. Use whichever feels natural!

questions.text: Which is the correct way to ask someone's opinion about coffee?
questions.explanation: 'Mitä mieltä sinä olet [noun]-sta/-stä?' is the standard question form. It means 'What do you think about [noun]?'

questions.text: Ask your friend 'What do you think about Turku?' Use the proper question format.
questions.explanation: 'Mitä mieltä olet Turusta?' is the correct form. The object takes -sta/-stä suffix to match the question format.

questions.text: What does 'Onko Turku sinun mielestä kiva kaupunki?' mean?
options: Where is Turku in your opinion?
Is Turku in your opinion a nice city?
What is Turku called in your opinion?
questions.explanation: This is an alternative way to ask for opinion: 'Onko [noun] sinun mielestä [adjective]?' = 'Is [noun] in your opinion [adjective]?'

questions.text: Complete the sentence: 'In our opinion, exercise is important.' Use the -sta/-stä method.
questions.explanation: Me → meistä (adding -sta/-stä). 'Meistä liikunta on tärkeää' = In our opinion, exercise is important.

questions.text: Which pair correctly shows the difference between COLOR and OPINION?
options: Musta auto = the car's color; Musta auto on kaunis = just the color
Musta auto on kaunis = black car is beautiful; Musta auto on kaunis = in my opinion the car is beautiful (both are the same)
Musta auto = black car; Musta kahvi on hyvää = in my opinion, coffee is good (different meanings based on context)
Musta = only black, never means opinion
questions.explanation: Context determines meaning! 'Musta auto' = the car is black. 'Musta kahvi on hyvää' = in my opinion, coffee is good. The grammar is identical.

questions.text: When asking 'What is your favorite food?', what is the Finnish word for 'favorite'?
questions.explanation: 'Lempi' means 'favorite' when used in compounds. 'Mikä sinun lempiruoka on?' = What is your favorite food? Other lempi compounds: lempijuoma (favorite drink).
